CRITICAL_bucket_change.old_bucket_status: DEAD since 2025-09-01: every request (list, HEAD, GET, path-style, v1) returns 403 AccessDenied as of 2026-06-10
CRITICAL_bucket_change.auth: anonymous works for ListObjectsV2, GetObject, and Range GETs (botocore Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED))

archive_bucket_layout.prefix_structure: YYYY/MM/DD/SSSS/  (4-digit year, zero-padded month/day, 4-char ICAO uppercase)
archive_bucket_layout.timestamp_meaning: volume scan START time, UTC, encoded in the filename; verified against the 24-byte Archive II header (julian date/ms fields decoded to the same 2026-06-10 21:29:28.502 UTC)
archive_bucket_layout.V06_vs_V08: ALL 45 sites uploading _V08 today are TDWR terminal radars (T-prefix: TOKC, TJFK, TDFW, ...). All 157 WSR-88D sites (K*, P*, RKSG, TJUA, LPLA) upload _V06. TJUA is a WSR-88D despite the T prefix and uploads _V06.
archive_bucket_layout.MDM_files: Model Data Message files, ~680-714 KB, roughly hourly (21 for KTLX today vs 213 volumes). NOT radar volumes - filter them out. Suffix is appended AFTER the version: _V06_MDM.

volume_file_sizes_KTLX_today.note: ~4-7 MB per volume in quiet weather, VCP 35

archive_bucket_layout.cadence_KTLX_today: ~7 min between volumes (VCP 35 clear-air); VCP 12/212 in severe weather is ~4.5 min
archive_bucket_layout.upload_lag: object appears ~7 min after the filename timestamp (i.e., when the volume COMPLETES); LastModified - filename_time ~= volume duration

recipe: 1. d = today UTC; list_objects_v2(Bucket='unidata-nexrad-level2', Prefix=f'{d:%Y/%m/%d}/{site}/') with paginator
2. keep keys matching suffix _V06 or _V08 exactly (reject _MDM and anything with a further extension like _V08.001)
3. lexicographic max of remaining keys IS the newest (zero-padded timestamps sort correctly)
4. if today's prefix is empty (just after 00Z, or site down), fall back to yesterday's prefix
5. any object present is a COMPLETE volume - partials are never visible in the archive bucket; staleness is at most one volume duration (~4.5-7 min) plus upload
latest_complete_volume_logic.realtime_alternative: unidata-nexrad-level2-chunks has per-volume chunks: SSSS/VVV/YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS-CCC-T where VVV = 3-digit rolling volume number (wraps; KTLX showed 327-336+), CCC = chunk sequence, T = S(start)/I(intermediate)/E(end). Old volume dirs persist for days, so 'latest' requires comparing chunk timestamps, not dir numbers. Not needed for a metadata-only tool.

station_table.status: HTTP 200, text/plain, 40916 bytes, 212 lines (header + dash ruler + 210 data rows)
station_table.format: fixed-width; derive column spans from the dash ruler on line 2
station_table.units: LAT/LON decimal degrees (negative = W); ELEV is FEET (KABX=5951 ft matches Albuquerque radar ~1789 m); UTC is offset hours
parsing_gotchas: ST and COUNTY are blank for overseas sites (LPLA Azores, RKJK/RKSG Korea, RODN Japan) - parser must tolerate empty fields
filter STNTYPE=='NEXRAD' for WSR-88D nearest-site lookup; do NOT filter by ICAO first letter (TJUA is NEXRAD, T-prefix)
all 210 rows have parseable floats for LAT/LON (verified)
LON column is 10 chars so precision is ~5-6 decimals

level2_header_reading_without_pyart.volume_header_24_bytes: Range GET bytes=0-23: struct '>9s3sII4s' -> tape ('AR2V0006.'), extension number ('745'), days since 1970-01-01 (+1), ms since midnight, ICAO. Verified on live KTLX volume.
level2_header_reading_without_pyart.cheap_VCP_extraction: bytes 24-27 = big-endian int32 compressed size of the metadata LDM record (2356 bytes observed); Range GET that many bytes from offset 28; bz2.decompress -> 134 messages x 2432 bytes; message type at byte offset 15 of each 2432-byte message; message type 5 (VCP data) carries pattern number at data offset +4 (bytes 32-34 of the message). Verified: returned VCP 35, matching MetPy. Total download ~2.4 KB.
level2_header_reading_without_pyart.metpy_level2file: metpy.io.Level2File (ALREADY in venv) parses the FULL volume: 1.6 s download (6.4 MB) + 1.8 s parse for KTLX. Gives f.stid, f.dt, f.vcp_info.num (35), f.sweeps (12 sweeps). Moments per ray at ray[4] dict keyed by bytes (b'REF', b'ZDR', b'PHI', b'RHO', b'CFP'); ray[4][b'REF'] = (DataBlockHdr(num_gates=1832, first_gate=2.125, gate_width=0.25, ...), float64 ndarray with NaN gaps). Max REF today: 37.5 dBZ. Emits harmless 'Unknown message: 32' warning.
level2_header_reading_without_pyart.verdict: NO Py-ART or nexradaws needed. VCP: ~2.4 KB via manual range-GET parse, or full MetPy parse. Max dBZ: requires full volume download + MetPy parse (~3.5 s total).
